# TanStack Query

TanStack Query (formerly React Query) is an asynchronous state management library designed to simplify fetching, caching, synchronizing, and updating server state in web applications. It provides a powerful, protocol-agnostic data fetching solution that works seamlessly with REST, GraphQL, or any Promise-based API, eliminating the need for manual cache management while offering automatic background refetching, pagination, infinite scroll support, and optimistic updates.

The library is framework-agnostic at its core (`@tanstack/query-core`) with official adapters for React, Vue, Solid, Svelte, Angular, and Preact. It handles complex data synchronization scenarios including dependent queries, parallel queries, query invalidation, and offline support. TanStack Query's smart caching strategy with configurable stale times and garbage collection makes it ideal for building responsive, data-driven applications that feel instantaneous to users.

## QueryClient

The QueryClient is the central cache manager that all queries and mutations interact with. It provides methods for prefetching, invalidating, and manually updating cached data.

```tsx
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from '@tanstack/react-query'

// Create a client with default options
const queryClient = new QueryClient({
  defaultOptions: {
    queries: {
      staleTime: 1000 * 60 * 5, // 5 minutes
      gcTime: 1000 * 60 * 30, // 30 minutes (garbage collection)
      retry: 3,
      refetchOnWindowFocus: true,
    },
    mutations: {
      retry: 1,
    },
  },
})

// Wrap your app with the provider
function App() {
  return (
    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>
      <YourApp />
    </QueryClientProvider>
  )
}
```

## useQuery

The primary hook for fetching and caching data. It automatically handles loading states, errors, caching, and background refetching based on the provided query key and fetch function.

```tsx
import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function TodoList() {
  const {
    data,
    isPending,
    isError,
    error,
    isFetching,
    refetch,
  } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['todos'],
    queryFn: async () => {
      const response = await fetch('/api/todos')
      if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Network response was not ok')
      return response.json()
    },
    staleTime: 1000 * 60, // Consider data fresh for 1 minute
    refetchInterval: 1000 * 30, // Refetch every 30 seconds
  })

  if (isPending) return <span>Loading...</span>
  if (isError) return <span>Error: {error.message}</span>

  return (
    <div>
      {isFetching && <span>Updating...</span>}
      <ul>
        {data.map((todo) => (
          <li key={todo.id}>{todo.title}</li>
        ))}
      </ul>
      <button onClick={() => refetch()}>Refresh</button>
    </div>
  )
}
```

## useQuery with Query Keys

Query keys uniquely identify cached data and support hierarchical structures. Changes to the key automatically trigger new fetches, enabling dependent and parameterized queries.

```tsx
import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function TodoDetail({ todoId }) {
  // Query key as array with parameters
  const { data, isPending } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['todos', todoId],
    queryFn: async () => {
      const response = await fetch(`/api/todos/${todoId}`)
      return response.json()
    },
    // Only fetch when todoId is truthy
    enabled: !!todoId,
  })

  // Query key with object for complex filters
  const { data: filteredTodos } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['todos', { status: 'completed', page: 1 }],
    queryFn: async ({ queryKey }) => {
      const [, { status, page }] = queryKey
      const response = await fetch(`/api/todos?status=${status}&page=${page}`)
      return response.json()
    },
  })

  if (isPending) return <span>Loading...</span>
  return <div>{data.title}</div>
}
```

## useMutation

Used for creating, updating, or deleting data. Mutations provide callbacks for handling success, error, and settlement states, making them ideal for optimistic updates and cache invalidation.

```tsx
import { useMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function AddTodo() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()

  const mutation = useMutation({
    mutationFn: async (newTodo) => {
      const response = await fetch('/api/todos', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify(newTodo),
      })
      if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to create todo')
      return response.json()
    },
    onSuccess: (data) => {
      // Invalidate and refetch todos list
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
      console.log('Todo created:', data)
    },
    onError: (error) => {
      console.error('Error creating todo:', error.message)
    },
    onSettled: () => {
      // Runs on both success and error
      console.log('Mutation completed')
    },
    retry: 3,
  })

  return (
    <div>
      <button
        onClick={() => mutation.mutate({ title: 'New Todo', completed: false })}
        disabled={mutation.isPending}
      >
        {mutation.isPending ? 'Adding...' : 'Add Todo'}
      </button>
      {mutation.isError && <p>Error: {mutation.error.message}</p>}
      {mutation.isSuccess && <p>Todo added!</p>}
    </div>
  )
}
```

## useMutation with Async/Await

The `mutateAsync` function returns a Promise, enabling try/catch handling and sequential operations.

```tsx
import { useMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function TodoForm() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()

  const createTodo = useMutation({
    mutationFn: async (newTodo) => {
      const response = await fetch('/api/todos', {
        method: 'POST',
        body: JSON.stringify(newTodo),
      })
      return response.json()
    },
  })

  const handleSubmit = async (e) => {
    e.preventDefault()
    const formData = new FormData(e.target)

    try {
      const result = await createTodo.mutateAsync({
        title: formData.get('title'),
        completed: false,
      })
      console.log('Created todo with id:', result.id)
      await queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
      e.target.reset()
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Failed to create todo:', error)
    }
  }

  return (
    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
      <input name="title" required />
      <button type="submit" disabled={createTodo.isPending}>
        {createTodo.isPending ? 'Saving...' : 'Create'}
      </button>
    </form>
  )
}
```

## Query Invalidation

Manually mark queries as stale and trigger refetches. Invalidation supports partial key matching and predicate functions for fine-grained control.

```tsx
import { useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function CacheControls() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()

  const invalidateAll = () => {
    // Invalidate all queries
    queryClient.invalidateQueries()
  }

  const invalidateTodos = () => {
    // Invalidate all queries starting with 'todos'
    queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
  }

  const invalidateExact = () => {
    // Invalidate only exact match
    queryClient.invalidateQueries({
      queryKey: ['todos', { status: 'completed' }],
      exact: true,
    })
  }

  const invalidateWithPredicate = () => {
    // Custom predicate for complex matching
    queryClient.invalidateQueries({
      predicate: (query) =>
        query.queryKey[0] === 'todos' &&
        query.queryKey[1]?.priority === 'high',
    })
  }

  const refetchActive = async () => {
    // Refetch all active queries matching key
    await queryClient.refetchQueries({
      queryKey: ['todos'],
      type: 'active',
    })
  }

  return (
    <div>
      <button onClick={invalidateAll}>Invalidate All</button>
      <button onClick={invalidateTodos}>Invalidate Todos</button>
      <button onClick={invalidateExact}>Invalidate Exact</button>
      <button onClick={refetchActive}>Refetch Active</button>
    </div>
  )
}
```

## useInfiniteQuery

Handles paginated and infinite scroll data fetching. Manages multiple pages of data with built-in support for loading more content in either direction.

```tsx
import { useInfiniteQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function InfiniteProjectList() {
  const {
    data,
    error,
    fetchNextPage,
    fetchPreviousPage,
    hasNextPage,
    hasPreviousPage,
    isFetchingNextPage,
    isFetchingPreviousPage,
    status,
  } = useInfiniteQuery({
    queryKey: ['projects'],
    queryFn: async ({ pageParam }) => {
      const response = await fetch(`/api/projects?cursor=${pageParam}&limit=10`)
      return response.json()
    },
    initialPageParam: 0,
    getNextPageParam: (lastPage) => lastPage.nextCursor ?? undefined,
    getPreviousPageParam: (firstPage) => firstPage.prevCursor ?? undefined,
    maxPages: 5, // Limit stored pages for memory optimization
  })

  if (status === 'pending') return <p>Loading...</p>
  if (status === 'error') return <p>Error: {error.message}</p>

  return (
    <div>
      <button
        onClick={() => fetchPreviousPage()}
        disabled={!hasPreviousPage || isFetchingPreviousPage}
      >
        {isFetchingPreviousPage ? 'Loading...' : 'Load Previous'}
      </button>

      {data.pages.map((page, i) => (
        <div key={i}>
          {page.data.map((project) => (
            <p key={project.id}>{project.name}</p>
          ))}
        </div>
      ))}

      <button
        onClick={() => fetchNextPage()}
        disabled={!hasNextPage || isFetchingNextPage}
      >
        {isFetchingNextPage ? 'Loading more...' : hasNextPage ? 'Load More' : 'No more data'}
      </button>
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Optimistic Updates

Update the UI immediately before the mutation completes, then rollback on error. This pattern provides instant feedback for better user experience.

```tsx
import { useMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function OptimisticTodoList() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()

  const updateTodo = useMutation({
    mutationFn: async (updatedTodo) => {
      const response = await fetch(`/api/todos/${updatedTodo.id}`, {
        method: 'PUT',
        body: JSON.stringify(updatedTodo),
      })
      return response.json()
    },
    onMutate: async (newTodo, context) => {
      // Cancel outgoing refetches to prevent overwriting optimistic update
      await context.client.cancelQueries({ queryKey: ['todos', newTodo.id] })

      // Snapshot previous value for rollback
      const previousTodo = context.client.getQueryData(['todos', newTodo.id])

      // Optimistically update cache
      context.client.setQueryData(['todos', newTodo.id], newTodo)

      // Return context for rollback
      return { previousTodo }
    },
    onError: (err, newTodo, result, context) => {
      // Rollback on error
      context.client.setQueryData(['todos', newTodo.id], result.previousTodo)
    },
    onSettled: (data, error, variables, result, context) => {
      // Always refetch to ensure cache consistency
      context.client.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos', variables.id] })
    },
  })

  const handleToggle = (todo) => {
    updateTodo.mutate({ ...todo, completed: !todo.completed })
  }

  return (
    <ul>
      {todos.map((todo) => (
        <li key={todo.id}>
          <input
            type="checkbox"
            checked={todo.completed}
            onChange={() => handleToggle(todo)}
          />
          {todo.title}
        </li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  )
}
```

## Prefetching

Load data before it's needed to eliminate loading states. Useful for hover states, route transitions, or predictive loading patterns.

```tsx
import { useQuery, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function ArticleList({ articles }) {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()

  const prefetchArticle = async (articleId) => {
    await queryClient.prefetchQuery({
      queryKey: ['article', articleId],
      queryFn: async () => {
        const response = await fetch(`/api/articles/${articleId}`)
        return response.json()
      },
      staleTime: 1000 * 60 * 5, // Consider fresh for 5 minutes
    })
  }

  return (
    <ul>
      {articles.map((article) => (
        <li
          key={article.id}
          onMouseEnter={() => prefetchArticle(article.id)}
          onFocus={() => prefetchArticle(article.id)}
        >
          <a href={`/articles/${article.id}`}>{article.title}</a>
        </li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  )
}

// Prefetch in event handlers or route loaders
async function prefetchRouteData(queryClient, articleId) {
  await queryClient.prefetchQuery({
    queryKey: ['article', articleId],
    queryFn: () => fetchArticle(articleId),
  })
}
```

## useSuspenseQuery

Integrates with React Suspense for declarative loading states. Data is guaranteed to be defined when the component renders, simplifying TypeScript types.

```tsx
import { useSuspenseQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'
import { Suspense } from 'react'
import { ErrorBoundary } from 'react-error-boundary'
import { QueryErrorResetBoundary } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function TodoListSuspense() {
  // data is guaranteed to be defined (no undefined check needed)
  const { data } = useSuspenseQuery({
    queryKey: ['todos'],
    queryFn: async () => {
      const response = await fetch('/api/todos')
      return response.json()
    },
  })

  return (
    <ul>
      {data.map((todo) => (
        <li key={todo.id}>{todo.title}</li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  )
}

function App() {
  return (
    <QueryErrorResetBoundary>
      {({ reset }) => (
        <ErrorBoundary
          onReset={reset}
          fallbackRender={({ resetErrorBoundary, error }) => (
            <div>
              <p>Error: {error.message}</p>
              <button onClick={resetErrorBoundary}>Retry</button>
            </div>
          )}
        >
          <Suspense fallback={<div>Loading todos...</div>}>
            <TodoListSuspense />
          </Suspense>
        </ErrorBoundary>
      )}
    </QueryErrorResetBoundary>
  )
}
```

## setQueryData

Directly update cached data without refetching. Useful for synchronous updates when you already have the data available.

```tsx
import { useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function DirectCacheUpdate() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()

  // Set data directly
  const setTodos = (todos) => {
    queryClient.setQueryData(['todos'], todos)
  }

  // Update with function (access previous data)
  const addTodo = (newTodo) => {
    queryClient.setQueryData(['todos'], (oldTodos) => {
      if (!oldTodos) return [newTodo]
      return [...oldTodos, newTodo]
    })
  }

  // Update specific item
  const updateTodo = (updatedTodo) => {
    queryClient.setQueryData(['todos'], (oldTodos) =>
      oldTodos?.map((todo) =>
        todo.id === updatedTodo.id ? updatedTodo : todo
      )
    )
  }

  // Remove item
  const removeTodo = (todoId) => {
    queryClient.setQueryData(['todos'], (oldTodos) =>
      oldTodos?.filter((todo) => todo.id !== todoId)
    )
  }

  // Get current cached data
  const getTodos = () => {
    return queryClient.getQueryData(['todos'])
  }

  // Ensure data exists (fetch if missing)
  const ensureTodos = async () => {
    return queryClient.ensureQueryData({
      queryKey: ['todos'],
      queryFn: fetchTodos,
    })
  }
}
```

## Dependent Queries

Fetch data that depends on the result of another query. Use the `enabled` option to control when queries execute.

```tsx
import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'

function UserPosts({ userId }) {
  // First query: fetch user
  const { data: user, isPending: isUserPending } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['user', userId],
    queryFn: async () => {
      const response = await fetch(`/api/users/${userId}`)
      return response.json()
    },
  })

  // Dependent query: fetch posts only after user is loaded
  const { data: posts, isPending: isPostsPending } = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['posts', user?.id],
    queryFn: async () => {
      const response = await fetch(`/api/users/${user.id}/posts`)
      return response.json()
    },
    // Only run when user data is available
    enabled: !!user?.id,
  })

  if (isUserPending) return <div>Loading user...</div>
  if (isPostsPending) return <div>Loading posts...</div>

  return (
    <div>
      <h1>{user.name}'s Posts</h1>
      <ul>
        {posts.map((post) => (
          <li key={post.id}>{post.title}</li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Parallel Queries

Fetch multiple independent queries simultaneously using multiple `useQuery` hooks or `useQueries` for dynamic query lists.

```tsx
import { useQuery, useQueries } from '@tanstack/react-query'

// Multiple useQuery hooks run in parallel automatically
function Dashboard() {
  const usersQuery = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['users'],
    queryFn: fetchUsers,
  })

  const projectsQuery = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['projects'],
    queryFn: fetchProjects,
  })

  const statsQuery = useQuery({
    queryKey: ['stats'],
    queryFn: fetchStats,
  })

  const isLoading = usersQuery.isPending || projectsQuery.isPending || statsQuery.isPending

  if (isLoading) return <div>Loading dashboard...</div>

  return (
    <div>
      <UsersList users={usersQuery.data} />
      <ProjectsList projects={projectsQuery.data} />
      <StatsPanel stats={statsQuery.data} />
    </div>
  )
}

// Dynamic parallel queries with useQueries
function MultipleUserProfiles({ userIds }) {
  const userQueries = useQueries({
    queries: userIds.map((userId) => ({
      queryKey: ['user', userId],
      queryFn: () => fetchUserById(userId),
      staleTime: 1000 * 60 * 5,
    })),
    combine: (results) => ({
      data: results.map((result) => result.data),
      pending: results.some((result) => result.isPending),
      error: results.find((result) => result.error)?.error,
    }),
  })

  if (userQueries.pending) return <div>Loading users...</div>
  if (userQueries.error) return <div>Error: {userQueries.error.message}</div>

  return (
    <ul>
      {userQueries.data.map((user) => (
        <li key={user?.id}>{user?.name}</li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  )
}
```

## queryOptions Helper

Create reusable, type-safe query configurations that can be shared across components and used with QueryClient methods.

```tsx
import { queryOptions, useQuery, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

// Define reusable query options
const todosQueryOptions = queryOptions({
  queryKey: ['todos'],
  queryFn: async () => {
    const response = await fetch('/api/todos')
    return response.json()
  },
  staleTime: 1000 * 60 * 5,
})

const todoQueryOptions = (todoId: number) =>
  queryOptions({
    queryKey: ['todos', todoId],
    queryFn: async () => {
      const response = await fetch(`/api/todos/${todoId}`)
      return response.json()
    },
    enabled: !!todoId,
  })

// Use in components
function TodoList() {
  const { data } = useQuery(todosQueryOptions)
  return <ul>{data?.map((todo) => <li key={todo.id}>{todo.title}</li>)}</ul>
}

function TodoDetail({ id }) {
  const { data } = useQuery(todoQueryOptions(id))
  return <div>{data?.title}</div>
}

// Use with QueryClient methods
function PrefetchExample() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()

  const prefetchTodos = () => {
    queryClient.prefetchQuery(todosQueryOptions)
  }

  const prefetchTodo = (id: number) => {
    queryClient.prefetchQuery(todoQueryOptions(id))
  }

  const invalidateTodos = () => {
    queryClient.invalidateQueries(todosQueryOptions)
  }
}
```

## Global Query Defaults

Set default options for specific query keys using `setQueryDefaults`. Useful for centralizing configuration.

```tsx
import { QueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

const queryClient = new QueryClient()

// Set defaults for all 'todos' queries
queryClient.setQueryDefaults(['todos'], {
  queryFn: async () => {
    const response = await fetch('/api/todos')
    return response.json()
  },
  staleTime: 1000 * 60 * 5,
  gcTime: 1000 * 60 * 30,
})

// Set defaults for mutations
queryClient.setMutationDefaults(['addTodo'], {
  mutationFn: async (newTodo) => {
    const response = await fetch('/api/todos', {
      method: 'POST',
      body: JSON.stringify(newTodo),
    })
    return response.json()
  },
  onSuccess: () => {
    queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] })
  },
})

// Now components can use simplified syntax
function SimpleTodoList() {
  // No need to specify queryFn - uses default
  const { data } = useQuery({ queryKey: ['todos'] })

  // Uses default mutationFn and onSuccess
  const { mutate } = useMutation({ mutationKey: ['addTodo'] })

  return (
    <div>
      <ul>{data?.map((todo) => <li key={todo.id}>{todo.title}</li>)}</ul>
      <button onClick={() => mutate({ title: 'New Todo' })}>Add</button>
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Summary

TanStack Query excels at managing server state in modern web applications by providing a declarative, cache-first approach to data fetching. The library handles common challenges like deduplicating requests, background updates, stale-while-revalidate patterns, pagination, and optimistic updates out of the box. Its query key system enables precise cache invalidation, while mutations with callbacks provide flexible patterns for keeping UI synchronized with server state.

The library integrates seamlessly with React Suspense for cleaner loading states, supports prefetching for instant navigation experiences, and offers fine-grained control over caching behavior through stale times and garbage collection. Whether building a simple CRUD application or complex real-time dashboards, TanStack Query provides the tools needed to deliver responsive user experiences while reducing boilerplate code. The framework-agnostic core means the same patterns work across React, Vue, Solid, Svelte, and Angular, making it a versatile choice for teams working with multiple frontend technologies.
